name: Tuna Braised with Cranberries, Port, and Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Dust the tuna steaks lightly with flour.
2. Melt half the butter over medium-high heat in a pan.
3. Pan-fry the tuna for 1 minute on each side or until browned. Remove the fish and increase the heat to high.
4. Deglaze the pan by pouring in the port wine, adding shallots, cranberry sauce, demi-glaze/brown sauce, and orange zest. Bring to a boil.
5. Reduce the heat to simmer and add cream. Return the tuna steaks to braise for 4 minutes.
6. Remove the tuna to serving plates and reduce the sauce an additional 2-3 minutes.
7. Swirl in the remaining butter and pour the sauce over the tuna. Serves 2.
Note: Demi-glaze is a refinement on brown sauce used by professional chefs, available in gourmet sections or as homemade/quality commercial alternatives. For non-gourmet cooks, substitute with good-quality beef stock (not bouillon) or chicken stock, ensuring low-salt content. The common thread among these sauces is the strong meat flavor and minimal other ingredients.
